data.title: Amazon (AMZN) Surpasses Walmart (WMT) in Revenue, but AWS Is the Real Growth Engine
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) has officially become the biggest global company by revenue, moving just ahead of Walmart (WMT). Walmart reported sales of $713.2 billion for the year ending January 31. Meanwhile, Amazon reported $717 billion in sales for 2025, using its December fiscal year. This shift highlights how much Amazon has grown from its early days as an online bookseller into a company with a reach across retail and tech.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, the story is not only about online shopping. Over the past decade, Amazon's revenue has grown much faster than Walmart's, helped by both e-commerce growth and its large cloud unit. Amazon remains the top online retailer, while Walmart continues to lead in physical retail with more than 10,000 stores worldwide.

Meanwhile, AMZN shares rose 1.81% on Wednesday, closing at $204.79.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall on Walmart's Dismal Earnings Outlook, Geopolitical Tensions
data.description:
U.S. stock futures declined on Thursday morning after retail giant Walmart (WMT) announced a disappointing earnings outlook. Also, geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran weighed on investor sentiment. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.47%, 0.30%, and 0.27%, respectively, at 8:30 a.m. EST on February 19.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed higher. The S&P 500 and the Dow Jones advanced 0.56% and 0.26%, respectively, while the Nasdaq rose 0.78%. Gains in Magnificent Seven stocks, particularly Nvidia (NVDA) and Amazon (AMZN), alongside financials and energy sectors, propelled the broader rally despite ongoing pressures.

On Wednesday, geopolitical tensions drove oil prices up over 4% after Vice President JD Vance stated Iran had not met U.S. demands in nuclear talks, warning President Trump might use military force if needed. Additionally, the Federal Reserve's January minutes revealed divided views on future interest rates.

Turning to trending stocks, Walmart stock was down about 3% in Thursday's pre-market trading after the retailer's weak earnings outlook overshadowed better-than-expected Q4 results. Meanwhile, Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) stock rose 4% after the telehealth company announced a $1.15 billion deal to acquire Australian digital health company Eucalyptus. 

Etsy (ETSY) stock rallied 21% after the online marketplace announced a deal to sell its Depop fashion marketplace to eBay (EBAY) for $1.2 billion. Also, Etsy announced mixed fourth-quarter results, with earnings exceeding expectations but revenue lagging estimates.

In contrast, Carvana (CVNA) stock tanked 9% after the online used-car retailer missed the Street's Q4 adjusted EBITDA expectations. Also, Wayfair (W) stock fell 4% in pre-market trading despite the furniture and home goods retailer reporting its first annual sales growth since 2020 and Q4 results beating estimates.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: BitMine Stock Positioned for a Rebound as Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, and BlackRock Buy More BMNR Shares
data.description:
BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) is seeing a massive surge in interest from some of the most powerful names on Wall Street. Even though the stock price has struggled lately, Morgan Stanley (MS) has sent a clear message by increasing its stake by 26%. This brings its total holdings to over 12.1 million shares, worth roughly $331 million. This aggressive buying suggests that the world's largest investment firms are looking past the current market noise and focusing on BitMine's massive digital asset treasury.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Institutional Support Creates a Solid Price Floor

Morgan Stanley isn't the only giant stepping in to support the stock. BlackRock (BLK) increased its position by a staggering 166%, and Goldman Sachs (GS) raised its stake by 588%. This level of institutional support helps create a floor for the stock price. When these massive firms buy millions of shares, it makes the company more stable and less likely to fall further. Their continued buying shows that they view BitMine as a long-term winner rather than a short-term gamble.

BitMine's Massive Ethereum Holdings Drive Future Value

The company's decision to keep buying Ethereum (ETH-USD) is a major reason for this institutional confidence. BitMine recently added another 45,759 Ether to its collection, bringing its total holdings to 4.37 million ETH. This treasure chest is worth billions and provides a real, physical value to the stock. Because the stock price is backed by so much Ethereum, it acts as a safety net. If the price of Ethereum recovers in 2026 as many analysts predict, BitMine's stock is likely to follow it upward.

BitMine's Strong Asset Value Gives the Company an Edge

BitMine's ability to keep its mNAV ratio (the value of its assets compared to its stock price) above 1.0 is a huge advantage. Keeping this ratio healthy allows BitMine to raise more money without hurting its current stockholders. This gives the company the financial muscle to keep operating and growing even when the broader market is down. It proves that BitMine has enough assets to survive a market downturn while staying prepared for the next big rally.

Is BitMine Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, BMNR stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on two Buy ratings from analysts assigned in the last three months. The average 12-month BMNR price target sits at $43, implying an upside potential of 117%.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: SoundHound AI Earnings Are Near: 3 Numbers that Could Move SOUN Stock
data.description:
Earnings season is heating up, and SoundHound AI (SOUN) is set to report its latest results next week on February 26. For investors, the upcoming report could be a key moment, as a few critical numbers may determine SOUN stock's next move. From revenue growth to AI adoption metrics and profitability trends, certain figures will likely grab the most attention. Here's a look at three numbers that could have the biggest impact on SOUN stock and what they mean for investors ahead of the earnings release.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, SoundHound AI focuses on voice recognition and natural language processing, providing AI-powered solutions for multiple industries. Here are 3 key numbers that matter most.

1. Revenue / Top-Line Growth

Investors will closely watch total revenue and year-over-year growth. Strong growth indicates expanding adoption of SoundHound's AI voice and conversational platforms, while weak growth could raise concerns about market traction.

    For the full year, revenue is projected to reach $165 million–$180 million, up sharply from $84.7 million in 2024, highlighting the company's rapid expansion and growing market presence. This upward revision underscores the rising demand for SoundHound's voice AI solutions and its expanding product portfolio. While the company has yet to turn a profit, its rapid revenue growth and optimistic outlook signal strong long-term potential for investors.

    2. Adjusted Profit or Loss (EPS)

    Earnings per share (EPS), particularly adjusted EPS, reflect the company's profitability trends. If SoundHound posts a smaller-than-expected loss or a positive EPS surprise, it could trigger a bullish reaction in the stock. Conversely, losses that exceed expectations may put downward pressure on shares. Overall, Wall Street expects SoundHound AI to report a loss per share of $0.10, compared to $0.69 per share a year ago.

    In Q3 2025, SoundHound reported an adjusted net loss of $0.03 per share. This was better than analysts' expected loss of $0.09 and showed improvement from the $0.04 loss reported in the same quarter last year, signaling a gradual path toward profitability.

    3. 2026 Guidance

    Along with revenue and profit numbers, 2026 guidance will provide a forward-looking snapshot of the company's potential, helping investors decide whether to buy, hold, or reassess their position in SOUN stock after the earnings report.

    Strong guidance can reinforce confidence in SoundHound's AI products and market adoption, showing that management expects continued growth and traction across its platforms. Even if the company remains unprofitable, projections that indicate narrowing losses or improving margins provide investors with valuable insight into the path toward long-term profitability, helping them gauge the stock's potential and plan their investment strategy.

    Is SOUN a Good Stock to Buy?

    According to TipRanks, SOUN stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with five Buys and one Hold assigned in the last three months. The average SoundHound stock price target is $16.60, suggesting a potential upside of 120.16% from the current level.

    Year-to-date, SOUN stock is down by 24.4%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Quantum Computing News: INFQ Goes Public as LAES Posts 66% Growth and Quantum Stocks Turn Commercial
data.description:
Welcome to Thursday's edition of quantum computing news, as quantum stocks keep moving fast, and the story begins to center on who can build a real business today, as the bigger promise lies in the longer term. This week, several updates show how the space is starting to mix long-term AI-driven tech with near-term tools and funding. Let's begin!

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Infleqtion Joins the Public Market

Infleqtion Inc. (INFQ) began trading on the New York Stock Exchange after completing its merger with Churchill Capital Corp X. The firm is the first public company focused on neutral-atom systems. What stands out, though, is that Infleqtion also sells precision-sensing products like quantum clocks and inertial sensors. These tools already serve defense and space clients, including NASA and the U.S. Department of War. As a result, Infleqtion may have a steadier base than peers that rely only on future computing scale. Management also said it is "the only public company with commercial leadership across both quantum computing and precision sensing."

Quantum Moves Into Real Network Use

In another step forward, Classiq, Comcast (CMCSA), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) completed a trial using quantum methods to improve network routing. The project focused on finding backup paths during maintenance, which becomes harder as networks grow. Comcast said the work showed that "quantum computing for network optimization isn't theoretical, it's practical, scalable, and grounded in the needs of our customers." The trial also mixed quantum hardware runs with GPU-based simulation using AMD Instinct chips, showing how hybrid systems may lead the way in the near term.

Bigger Capital Flows Into Quantum Investing

Meanwhile, Quantonation Ventures, a Paris- and Boston-based venture capital fund focused on deep physics and quantum technologies, closed its second fund at €220 million, making it the largest dedicated quantum fund in the world. The firm said the goal is to move physics-based tech from labs into real infrastructure, including materials, sensing, and next-wave computing. Christophe Jurczak noted that "Quantum has spent decades being described as five years away," adding that the issue was not physics but building full ecosystems. This reflects a growing view that progress will come through steady system-building, not one sudden leap.

Post-Quantum Security Gains More Attention

We turn our attention to SEALSQ Corp (LAES), which reported FY2025 revenue of $18 million, up 66% year-over-year, with a large cash position of over $425 million. The company is focused on post-quantum chips, TPM products, and secure hardware tied to future encryption needs. It also expects revenue growth of 50% to 100% in FY2026. CEO Carlos Moreira said the firm is moving from investment mode into "a structured commercial acceleration phase." This shows how quantum-linked security may become one of the first areas to scale before full quantum machines arrive.

Tools Get Easier for Quantum Builders

We close this edition with PsiQuantum launching Circuit Designer as an open-access web tool meant to make quantum circuit diagrams easier to build and share. The company said the goal is to remove slow manual work that often means using vector apps or long LaTeX code. PsiQuantum said, "Circuit Designer transforms the most tedious part of quantum computing research into an efficient and exploratory process." The tool is built to handle circuits with hundreds of gates and supports sharing via links, PNGs, and SVGs, which may help teams move faster and keep work clear.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align the main publicly traded quantum stocks with the tickers mentioned in this piece. It is a simple way to see how firms like IonQ (IONQ) and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) stack up as public funding, lab gains, and capital market moves begin to align.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/19/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Dip Ahead of Walmart Results
data.description:
U.S. stock futures dipped early Thursday ahead of retail bellwether Walmart's (WMT) results. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.40%, 0.33%, and 0.36%, respectively, at 5:29 a.m. EST on February 19.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed higher. The S&P 500 advanced 0.56%, the Nasdaq rose 0.78%, and the Dow Jones gained 0.26%. Gains in Magnificent Seven tech stocks, particularly Nvidia (NVDA) and Amazon (AMZN), alongside financials and energy sectors, propelled the broader rally despite February pressures.

On Wednesday, geopolitical tensions drove oil prices up over 4% after Vice President JD Vance stated Iran had not met U.S. demands in nuclear talks, warning President Trump might use military force if needed. Additionally, the Federal Reserve's January minutes revealed divided views on future interest rates.

Turning to economic data, traders anticipate data on initial jobless claims and pending home sales. However, Friday's release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index for December, the Fed's favorite gauge, will remain the most awaited economic report.

On the earnings front, Walmart will report earnings before the bell, which could give clues about consumer spending. The retail giant has witnessed strong performance in 2026 so far and has recently moved into the $1 trillion market cap club.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.09%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $65.95 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $5,003 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower today amid earnings expectations from Airbus (EADSF) and Renault (RNSDF).

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded higher on February 19, with Mainland Chinese and Hong Kong markets remaining closed for the Lunar New Year.

Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei rose 0.57%, and the Topix gained 1.18%.

data.title: Why a Lone Hold Rating Appeared on Alibaba (BABA) Before Earnings
data.description:
E-commerce giant Alibaba (BABA) is expected to report Q3 FY26 results this month. While most Wall Street analysts remain bullish on the stock ahead of its upcoming results, one firm is stepping to the sidelines. Erste Group analyst Hans Engel downgraded Alibaba from Buy to Hold, becoming the only analyst in the latest coverage cycle without a Buy rating on the stock.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The move comes even as sentiment around Alibaba has improved due to its AI and cloud momentum.

Profitability Is the Real Concern

The analyst said Alibaba's AI strategy is a positive. Management is focused on generating revenue from AI through its cloud business. The company is also developing its own AI chips, which over time could make Alibaba less dependent on outside suppliers and strengthen its platform.

These efforts may improve the company's competitive position and support future growth as more businesses increase spending on AI computing.

That said, the analyst's main concern is profitability.

He noted that Alibaba's operating margin has declined in recent quarters. Heavy spending on technology and infrastructure, including AI projects, is weighing on earnings. The company's long-term liabilities have also increased, which could limit flexibility if growth slows.

As a result, he does not expect a sharp earnings recovery soon, even if sales remain stable. For now, he believes the stock is likely to trade sideways rather than deliver strong gains.

What Is the Price Target for BABA Stock?  

According to TipRanks, Alibaba stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with 15 Buys and one Hold rating assigned in the last three months. The average Alibaba price target of $203.09 implies about 30.38% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: DE Earnings: Deere Stock Leaps on Strong Q1 Beats
data.description:
Deere (DE) stock rallied on Thursday after the farm equipment company released its Fiscal Q1 2026 earnings report. The company started the report off strong with diluted earnings per share of $2.42. While this was down 24% year-over-year from $3.19, it still came in above Wall Street's estimate of $2.02 per share.

Deere reported revenue of $9.61 billion in its latest earnings report, which represents a 13% increase from the $8.51 billion reported in Fiscal Q1 2025. The company's revenue also surpassed the analysts' estimate of $7.59 billion for the quarter. Deere's revenue growth was fueled by a 3% increase in Production & Precision Ag net sales and a 24% increase in Small Ag & Turf net sales.

DE stock was up 5.69% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 1.26% rally yesterday. The shares have soared 27.43% year-to-date and 19.66% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Microsoft Stock Is Triggering a Bull Run as a 110% Surge in AI Contracts Creates a $625 Billion Revenue Stream
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is aggressively reclaiming its spot at the top of the tech world. While the stock faced a brief 15% slide earlier this year, the underlying numbers are now grabbing the market's attention. The company's total cloud revenue has soared past $51 billion, and the future earnings look even stronger. Microsoft has secured a staggering $625 billion in signed contracts that have yet to be billed. This massive wall of revenue represents a massive flood of guaranteed cash for the company.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Massive Backlog Creates a $625 Billion Engine of Growth

The Remaining Performance Obligation (RPO) is the single most dramatic number in Microsoft's portfolio. It represents over half a trillion dollars in guaranteed business waiting to hit the balance sheet. This figure exploded by 110% in the most recent update, largely fueled by its deep partnership with OpenAI. Microsoft is now the primary landlord for the world's most advanced AI research. Expecting to recognize $156 billion of that backlog as revenue within the next year gives the company a level of visibility and security that is almost unheard of in the tech sector.

Copilot Adoption Drives Growth

For the first time, Microsoft revealed that 15 million people are now paying for Microsoft 365 Copilot. While this represents 160% growth year-over-year, it is only the beginning of the story. Currently, only 3.3% of Microsoft's 450 million commercial users have upgraded. This low adoption rate fuels the bull case by leaving a massive runway for future profits. Converting just a small fraction of its remaining 435 million users could add billions in revenue without ever needing to find a new customer.

Microsoft's Strategic Spending Builds an Unbreakable Lead

The debate over Microsoft's $37.5 billion quarterly spending is shifting from risk to reward. CEO Satya Nadella has made it clear that the company is currently supply-constrained, meaning it cannot build data centers fast enough to keep up with customer demand. Building out custom AI chips and global infrastructure now ensures that Microsoft remains the primary landlord of the AI era. With AI service growth currently at 157%, the market is starting to view this spending as a necessary foundation for the next decade of dominance.

Is Microsoft a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, MSFT stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with 32 Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average Microsoft stock price target is $593.38, suggesting a potential upside of 48.5% from the current level.
data.author: Annika Masrani
